title: Predictive Indoor Climate AI Service
description: An intelligent microservice providing backend infrastructure for forecasting future indoor climate conditions (temperature and relative humidity) of a specific heritage building or room. Operates in two stages: (1) Offline Model Training — a historical dataset correlating indoor IoCT time-series data with outdoor weather station data is used to train and validate a building-specific time-series forecasting model (e.g. LSTM networks, Gradient Boosting Machines or other regression models), producing a highly-tuned predictive model capturing the unique thermal inertia, air permeability and moisture buffering capacity of the asset; (2) Online Prediction (Inference) — once deployed, the service takes a future weather forecast (e.g. for the next 24–72 hours) as primary input, feeds it into the trained model and returns a time-series forecast of indoor climate for the same period. Called by the RHDT on a regular schedule via standardised REST endpoints. Training performed on a high-performance workstation or cloud instance with powerful CPU and preferably high-VRAM GPU. Inference deployed as a lightweight Python microservice (Flask or FastAPI) on a standard cloud CPU VM (8–16 GB RAM).

access_points.notes: Python-based (Flask or FastAPI) or Node.js-based HTTP/HTTPS web server with JSON payload. Called by the RHDT on a regular schedule.

intended_for: Heritage building managers requiring proactive early-warning climate management
Conservation scientists needing evidence-based forecasts of environmental risk for sensitive collections
RHDT systems requiring predicted indoor climate data as input to the Risk Assessment Service

used_for.activity_label: operation_02 — Predictive Indoor Climate Forecasting: generating 24-hour indoor temperature and RH forecasts for the Japanese Tower using real-time IoT data and external weather forecast data as inputs
operation_04 — Risk Assessment and Alerting: providing short-term indoor climate forecasts as one of the three input data streams processed by the Risk Assessment Service

used_physical_object_of_type: High-performance workstation or cloud instance with powerful CPU and high-VRAM GPU (e.g. NVIDIA RTX series) for offline model training
Standard cloud-based CPU virtual machine or container with 8–16 GB RAM for online inference deployment
has_functionality: Offline training of a building-specific time-series forecasting model (LSTM, Gradient Boosting Machines or other regression models)
Online 24–72 hour indoor climate forecasting (temperature and RH)
REST API-based forecast delivery to the RHDT on a regular schedule
Support for early warning triggering and automated environmental control
Virtual 'what-if' testing of climate control strategies without endangering the real asset
